title: Aesthetics and Geometric Objects
description: Map variables to visual properties and choose the right geom.

slides.title: Common Aesthetics
slides.content:
Aesthetics define how variables are mapped to visual features. Common aesthetics:

- `x`, `y` – positions
- `color` – outline/stroke color
- `fill` – fill color (for bars, polygons)
- `size` – size of points/lines
- `alpha` – transparency
- `shape` – point shape
- `linetype` – line pattern

You set them inside `aes()` to map data to these properties.

slides.title: Mapping Aesthetics
slides.codeSnippet:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point()

quizData.question: Where do you put aesthetics that map data to visual properties?
quizData.explanation: Mappings go inside aes(). Constant aesthetics (e.g., color = "red") go outside aes().

slides.title: Geometric Objects (geoms)
slides.content:
Geoms determine the type of plot. Some common ones:

- `geom_point()` – scatterplot
- `geom_line()` – line plot
- `geom_smooth()` – smoothed conditional means
- `geom_bar()` – bar plot
- `geom_histogram()` – histogram
- `geom_boxplot()` – boxplot
- `geom_density()` – density plot

You can add multiple geoms to a single plot.

slides.title: Multiple Geoms
slides.content: Add points and a smooth line:
slides.codeSnippet:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")
